# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.
# Category: message_queue_security

Resources:
  MySharedEventQueueSQS:
    Type: "AWS::SQS::Queue"
    
  PermissivePolicyForAnyPrincipalAccessESPQUEUEACCESSPOINT1_08FB9754CBAE6D23BCBBDBECFAEDADDA-sqsqueueaccesspointvpclocal:PermissionsManager.AllowSendMessageToAll
    DependsOn: MySharedEventQueueSQS  # This is the Queue we're creating on top of this SNS topic, and not directly to be created here with a direct CloudFormation stack call or anything like that!... (AWS::Lambda::Function will create another resource) ...
    Permissions: SendMessage | ReceiveMessage  |  "effect=Allow"  # Allow the Lambda function permission for SQS Access. This is what actually sets up permssions on an individual service's endpoint, which isn’t possible through Cloudformation or direct calls to AWS Services at this stage!...
    Condition: SharedEventQueueLambdaFunctionCreated   # After lambda resource creation and PermissionsManager already set  ... (AWS::Lambda::Permission) not created yet. This is a placeholder for what actually happens in the real template before I can start building out actual permissions...... The next step will be to create this LAMBDA permission thingy after our Function has been defined...
